source: FEST Academic Handbook NOVEMBER 2025 KLUST 1.0 and ROF-05 Academic Dismissal Appeal Form

forms.purpose: Used by a student who wishes to appeal after academic dismissal.
forms.submission_deadline: Within the first two weeks of the semester
required_attachments: Academic Plan discussed with Academic Advisor
Supporting evidence/documents related to the appeal reason
student_acknowledgement_conditions: Abide by the Academic Plan discussed with the Academic Advisor
Improve academic performance to achieve a minimum CGPA of 2.00
Acknowledge that this is the only appeal to which the student is entitled
forms.post_approval_note: Academic Executive must forward this form to the Registrar’s Office for record-keeping and retain a copy for the Faculty.

forms.purpose: Used to withdraw from one or more registered courses after the add/drop period but before the withdrawal deadline.
forms.submission_window: After the first two weeks but before the thirteenth week of the normal semester; after the first week but before the seventh week of the short semester
forms.result_on_record: W grade appears on transcript but is not counted toward GPA
forms.official_date_rule: Official withdrawal date is the date the University receives the withdrawal form
notes: No withdrawal is permitted after the deadline except in extraordinary circumstances.
Withdrawing from a course does not remove outstanding fee obligations.

forms.purpose: Used to withdraw completely from the University.
forms.submission_window: Before the thirteenth week of the normal semester; before the seventh week of the short semester
notes: Withdrawal is not official until the form is submitted to the Registrar’s Office.
Failure to withdraw officially may affect readmission or recommendations.

forms.purpose: Used when a student wishes to postpone studies with valid reasons.
forms.deadline_limit: Not allowed after the twelfth week of the long semester or after the sixth week of the short semester
notes: Students are not permitted to postpone studies in order to undertake studies at another institution.
Returning students retain the same academic status as before postponement.
